structuralIssues.title: EN 13043 and EN 13055 AVCP mismatch with AGG
structuralIssues.description: EN 13043 (aggregates for bituminous mixtures) and EN 13055 (lightweight aggregates) are cross-listed in both RCP and AGG. RCP records AVCP as '2+' for both, while AGG records '2+, 4'. The correct value likely includes System 4 for certain characteristics. EN 13242 (unbound/hydraulically bound) is consistent across both families ('2+'). Name formats also differ slightly.
structuralIssues.action_required: Verify AVCP for EN 13043 and EN 13055 against the relevant Commission Decision. Update the family with incorrect data.

structuralIssues.title: Three content sections empty
structuralIssues.description: content.stakeholder_notes, content.key_risks, and content.sources_summary are empty.
structuralIssues.action_required: Populate with proposed content from deep-dive report §8.

structuralIssues.title: Eurobitume LCA 4.0 (2025) doubles bitumen GWP to 530 kg CO₂e/t — all DPP data must use updated figure
structuralIssues.description: Eurobitume LCA Report 4.0 (March 2025) updates paving-grade bitumen GWP100 from 216 kg CO₂e/t (LCA 3.1, 2021) to 530 kg CO₂e/t — a 145% increase. Change is due to improved methane accounting from crude oil extraction, not increased actual emissions. Data from 17 European refineries (75%+ coverage). Outputs are EN 15804+A2 compatible. ~70% of bitumen GWP stems from oil extraction and refining. All future RCP EPDs and DPP environmental declarations must use this updated figure. Previous EPDs using LCA 3.1 data are significantly understated.
structuralIssues.action_required: Flag Eurobitume LCA 4.0 GWP revision in content. Note EN 15804+A2 compatibility for DPP readiness.

structuralIssues.title: RAP recycling infrastructure provides DPP recycled content data — 45.5 Mt/yr available, 76% reused
structuralIssues.description: Reclaimed Asphalt Pavement (RAP) is Europe's most recycled construction material. 45.5 Mt available annually, 23.2 Mt utilised in asphalt production, 76% reused in mixes. 65-75% goes to new asphalt, 25-35% to unbound layers, ~5% to landfill. 100% recyclability demonstrated. RAP incorporation typically limited to 10-30% by national specifications. EN 13108-8 governs RAP specifications. This circular economy data is directly relevant to DPP recycled content and end-of-life data fields.
structuralIssues.source: EAPA Recycling (https://eapa.org/recycling/); EAPA Asphalt in Figures (https://eapa.org/asphalt-in-figures/); CAPRI RAP overview (https://www.capri-project.com/news-1/the-importance-of-re-userecycle-rap-quick-overview-in-europe)
structuralIssues.action_required: Document RAP circular economy data in content. Note as DPP recycled content exemplar.

structuralIssues.title: EU-27 asphalt 208.5 Mt (2024), ~$20.5B bitumen market — one of highest-volume CPR families
structuralIssues.description: EU-27 HMA+WMA production: 208.5 Mt (2024). European bitumen market: ~$20.48B (2024), road construction 85.5% of market. EU earmarked €7B for road modernisation (2024), Germany €2.8B/yr motorway spending. RCP is among the highest-tonnage CPR families. EAPA/Eurobitume coordinate as joint industry voice. EAPA Asphalt 4.0 explores BIM/digital twins for quality management — potential DPP infrastructure.
structuralIssues.source: EAPA Asphalt in Figures (https://eapa.org/asphalt-in-figures/); Market Data Forecast (https://www.marketdataforecast.com/market-reports/europe-bitumen-market); Mordor Intelligence (https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/europe-bitumen-market)
structuralIssues.action_required: Include market size context in content for DPP impact assessment.
